team_context.cap_situation: flush — ~$51M in cap space per OTC after massive 2026 free agency spending spree ($270M committed). Had league-leading $100M+ pre-FA. Still have room to maneuver.
team_context.trade_posture: Building around Cam Ward. New HC Robert Saleh, OC Brian Daboll, DC Gus Bradley installed. Just made 8+ FA signings. Hold #4 overall pick. Willing to move veterans who don't fit the timeline, but core young pieces are off limits. Likely buyers of future assets via draft but could move expiring vets for picks.

players.name: Cam Ward
players.performance_evidence: Struggled as a rookie on a bad team: 3,169 yards, 15 TD, 7 INT, 59.8% completion, 79.1 passer rating, 33.2 QBR (28th), 58.5 PFF overall grade (39th/43 QBs). Took a league-high 48+ sacks. However, improved significantly down the stretch — 10:1 TD:INT over final 8 games post-bye, set franchise rookie passing record. Shoulder injury Week 18 not expected to require surgery.

trade_value.reasoning: Franchise QB on a rookie deal with 3 years of team control plus 5th-year option. #1 overall pick one year ago. Despite rough rookie stats, showed clear improvement trajectory. The entire rebuild is built around him. His trade value in practice exceeds any single pick — multiple 1sts would be required. Expressed as pick 1 because he's the most valuable asset on the roster.

players.availability_reasoning: The franchise. Hired Robert Saleh and Brian Daboll specifically to develop him. Spent $270M in free agency building around him. Zero chance of a trade under any circumstances.
players.injury_notes: AC joint sprain in shoulder (Week 18, 2025). No surgery needed, expected 100% for 2026.

players.name: Mitchell Trubisky
players.performance_evidence: Career backup at this stage. Signed as veteran presence to mentor Ward and provide stability if Ward misses time. Familiar with Daboll's system from Buffalo. Has not been a meaningful starter since 2022.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran backup QB on a modest deal. Has some value as an experienced placeholder but 32 years old with limited upside. Worth a conditional late-round pick at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Backup QB signed specifically for Ward insurance. Would trade if a team offered a late pick, but no urgency to move him given he's the primary backup for their franchise QB.

players.name: Tony Pollard
players.performance_evidence: 1,082 rushing yards, 206 receiving yards, 5 TDs in 2025. Back-to-back 1,000-yard seasons in Tennessee. 4.5 YPC. PFF 67.0 overall (45th/55 HBs). Had a strong 3-game stretch with 367 yards and 3 TDs. Reliable but not explosive; production heavily dependent on O-line play. 50/50 snap split with Spears.

trade_value.reasoning: Expiring contract with zero guaranteed money left. Age 29 RB. Reliable but not special. Worth a 5th-round pick to a contender needing a workhorse, but the RB market is depressed. Titans could easily cut him with no dead cap.

players.availability_reasoning: Spotrac flagged him as a potential cut candidate. Contract has a built-in team out for 2026. Beat reporters note the team may be ready to 'flip the position over to younger/less expensive players.' If the Titans draft a RB at #4 (Jeremiyah Love buzz exists), Pollard becomes expendable immediately. Non-guaranteed $7.25M makes him a clean release or cheap trade chip.
players.injury_notes: Missed time in 2025. Generally durable across career but entering decline phase for RBs.
players.trade_buzz: Beat reporters have discussed his uncertain future. FA signings point toward potential #4 overall RB pick which would make Pollard redundant.

players.name: Tyjae Spears
players.performance_evidence: Only 283 rushing yards, 72 carries, 3.9 YPC in 2025 — a significant step back. Dealt with injuries (concussions in 2024, high-ankle sprain in 2025 preseason). Explosive in college at Tulane but hasn't translated to consistent NFL production as RB2 behind Pollard.

trade_value.reasoning: Final year of cheap rookie deal. Has flashed but hasn't produced consistently. Worth a 6th-round pick as a change-of-pace back with upside, but injury history and limited production cap value.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering final year of rookie deal. If Titans draft a RB early, Spears becomes the odd man out. Still young enough to interest teams looking for a backup with upside on a cheap contract.
players.injury_notes: Two concussions within two months in 2024. High-ankle sprain in 2025 preseason. Durability is a real concern.

players.name: Calvin Ridley
players.performance_evidence: Disastrous 2025: only 17 catches for 303 yards, 0 TDs in 7 games before a broken fibula ended his season in Week 11. Hamstring injury also sidelined him from Weeks 7-10. Titans actively tried to trade him at the deadline but found no takers due to the bloated contract. Has 3 career 1,000-yard seasons but none in Tennessee. Turns 32 in December.

trade_value.reasoning: Massively overpaid on a $92M deal. Coming off a broken fibula with only 303 yards in 2025. $26.5M cap hit makes him nearly untradeable. PFR estimated a trade would net maybe a 5th/6th-round pick IF the Titans ate significant salary. More likely a post-June 1 release candidate than a trade asset.

players.availability_reasoning: FOX Sports' Greg Auman listed him as a top-10 cap casualty. Titans actively shopped him at the 2025 trade deadline per multiple reports. Beat reporter Paul Kuharsky says the team may try to restructure or release. $13M dead cap on a release is manageable given their cap space. Expected to be cut or restructured before the 2026 season unless no adequate WR replacement is found.
players.injury_notes: Broken fibula (November 2025, season-ending). Hamstring injury earlier in 2025. Age-related decline concerns at 31.
players.character_notes: Year-long gambling suspension in 2022 is historical but no longer a current concern.
players.trade_buzz: Titans actively tried to trade him at 2025 deadline. Multiple reports indicate he's 'almost certainly gone.' Possible post-June 1 designation to save $18.7M.

players.name: Wan'Dale Robinson
players.performance_evidence: Career-high 1,014 yards and 4 TDs on 92 receptions in 2025 with Giants. 185 catches over last 2 seasons under Daboll. Set record for slot receiver AAV. Quick, reliable underneath target who excels on third downs. Not a traditional WR1 but an elite slot receiver.

trade_value.reasoning: Young (24), productive slot receiver on a new 4-year deal with team-friendly Year 1 cap hit. Perfect Daboll system fit. High guaranteed money ($40M) means the team is locked in. Worth a late 1st/early 2nd to a team that needs a reliable possession receiver, but the dead cap makes a trade impractical.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed days ago to a 4-year, $70-78M deal specifically to be Cam Ward's top target. Reunites with OC Daboll. $25M+ dead cap makes a trade financially impossible. Cornerstone of the offensive rebuild.
players.injury_notes: Has been relatively healthy over last two seasons. Minor injury history early in career.

players.name: Elic Ayomanor
players.performance_evidence: 34 catches for 428 yards and 3 TDs as a rookie in 14 games. Had promising early stretch — TDs in back-to-back weeks (Weeks 2-3). But production faded: topped 50 yards only twice all season. 12.6 yards/catch shows big-play ability. Dealt with hamstring issues.

trade_value.reasoning: Young Day 3 rookie with 3 years of cheap control. Showed flashes but inconsistent. Worth a 5th-round pick as a developmental WR with size (6-2, 206) and big-play ability on a minimum contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Part of the young core being developed around Ward. Cheap contract makes him easy to keep. Would only trade for a clear overpay, but he's not untouchable given the WR room additions (Robinson) and potential draft picks.
players.injury_notes: Hamstring injury caused him to miss time in 2025.

players.name: Gunnar Helm
players.performance_evidence: 44 catches for 357 yards and 2 TDs as a rookie. Emerged as TE1 over Chig Okonkwo (now departed) — averaged 5.0 targets and 4.2 catches per game over final 5 weeks. Sure-handed, solid blocker (70+ PFF pass blocking grades in preseason). Coaches raved about his toughness throughout camp.

trade_value.reasoning: Promising young TE on a cheap 4th-round rookie deal. Showed clear development curve as a rookie. With Okonkwo gone and Bellinger added as a blocking TE, Helm is positioned as the primary receiving TE. Worth a 4th-5th round pick but the team views him as a building block.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap, and developing. The Titans see him as TE1 going forward. Would only move him for a significant overpay. Bellinger was signed as a complementary piece, not a replacement.
players.injury_notes: Toe injury limited him late in 2025 season (missed Week 18).

players.name: Daniel Bellinger
players.performance_evidence: Signed from Giants to be in-line blocking TE in Daboll's system. Familiar with the offensive scheme from 3 years under Daboll in NY. Athletic, plus blocker. Not a high-volume receiver but a reliable piece. A to Z Sports noted he 'upgrades the Titans run blocking.'

trade_value.reasoning: Blocking TE on a fair contract familiar with the OC's system. $8M AAV is slightly rich for a TE2 but 3 years of control adds value. Worth a 4th-5th round pick, but high dead cap ($12M) makes a trade impractical in Year 1.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed to complement Helm. Dead cap makes a trade unrealistic in 2026. Would only move if a significantly better TE was acquired.

players.name: Dan Moore Jr.
players.performance_evidence: Inconsistent in first year with Titans. Led NFL in sacks allowed (12) in 2024 with Steelers, then continued to struggle in 2025 — allowed 2 sacks in a single game vs Chargers. Contract widely regarded as an overpay ($82M/4yr). Better than replacement level but not living up to the price tag. Spotrac noted he's 'fully guaranteed through 2026.'

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average starting LT on a bloated contract ($26.3M cap hit). Only 26, so there's time to develop, but the contract is a negative asset. Acquiring team would need to absorb a massive cap hit for mediocre play. Worth a 3rd-4th round pick if the Titans eat salary, but dead cap ($12.5M) makes a trade unlikely.

players.availability_reasoning: Committed through 2026 at minimum due to guarantees. Beat reporters say Borgonzi has 'enough on his to-do list without adding LT.' He'll start in 2026 barring an upgrade, but the Titans are stuck with the contract more than they chose it.

players.name: Peter Skoronski
players.performance_evidence: Showed real improvement in 2024 second half — PFF's league-high 88.2 pass-blocking grade from Week 12 onward. Season-long 76.3 pass-blocking grade was 7th among guards. Allowed just 4 sacks and 28 pressures in ~1,100 snaps. PFF ranked him 20th among guards entering 2025. Extension candidate — Spotrac values him at $19M/yr.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old former 1st-round guard on a cheap rookie deal with extension eligibility. Ascending player who dominated in pass protection late in 2024. 5th-year option decision looming. Worth a late 1st-round pick on talent and contract value, but the Titans need him to protect Ward.

players.availability_reasoning: Core OL piece protecting the franchise QB. Extension candidate valued at $19M/yr by Spotrac. Consecutive first-round OL picks (Skoronski/Latham) are the foundation. Moving him would undermine everything they're building.

players.name: Austin Schlottmann
players.performance_evidence: Signed from Giants where he had experience in Daboll's system. Journeyman center who provides scheme familiarity. Not a Pro Bowl talent but a reliable veteran plug at a position of need after Lloyd Cushenberry's release.

trade_value.reasoning: Replacement-level starting center on a modest deal. Brings system familiarity but limited upside. Worth a late-round pick swap at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Signed as a stop-gap. If the Titans draft a center or find an upgrade, Schlottmann becomes a backup. Fair value gets it done.

players.name: Garrett Dellinger
players.performance_evidence: 7th-round pick by Ravens in 2025, waived, claimed by Titans. Exclusive rights free agent re-signed for 2026. Depth interior lineman competing for starting RG spot after Kevin Zeitler's departure. Limited NFL track record.

trade_value.reasoning: ERFA on a minimum deal with minimal proven production. Essentially replacement-level depth. No meaningful trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece. Would trade for any pick offered, but unlikely anyone would call.

players.name: JC Latham
players.performance_evidence: Struggled at LT in 2024 (7 sacks allowed, 47 pressures). Moved to natural RT position in 2025 after Dan Moore signing. Was penalized 14 times in 2025 (5 holding, 6 false starts). Missed 4 games with hip injury and admitted he 'wasn't 100%' when he returned. Just 22 — raw but has the physical tools (6-6, 325).

trade_value.reasoning: Former top-7 pick, only 22, on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. Despite a rough start, his age and draft pedigree still carry significant value. Multiple teams would trade a 2nd-round pick for his upside. The Titans invested too much to give up now.

players.availability_reasoning: High draft pedigree and youth mean the Titans are committed to his development. Only 22 with 3 years of control. However, if a team offered a late 1st for him, the Titans might listen given his struggles. Not being shopped but has a price.
players.injury_notes: Hip injury caused him to miss 4 games in 2025. Expects full recovery with offseason rest.
players.character_notes: Penalty issues are a maturity/discipline concern, not character.

players.name: Oluwafemi Oladejo
players.performance_evidence: Only played 6 games as a rookie before a fractured leg ended his season in Week 6. Recorded 13 tackles and 2 TFLs. PFF grade was poor (48.2 overall) in limited action. Was the Titans' 2nd-round pick for his 'explosiveness and juice' transitioning from LB to EDGE. Too small a sample to evaluate properly.

trade_value.reasoning: 2nd-round pick with 3 years of control on a cheap deal, but missed most of his rookie year to injury and showed little in limited action. The draft pedigree and Saleh's scheme fit maintain value around a 3rd-4th round pick. The real value is in what he could become.

players.availability_reasoning: High draft pick the team is developing. EDGE is their biggest need — they wouldn't trade a potential answer there. But if someone offered a 2nd-round pick, they'd have to consider it given he's unproven.
players.injury_notes: Fractured leg in Week 6 of 2025. Designated to return from IR in December but couldn't make it back. Should be healthy for 2026.

players.name: Jordan Elliott
players.performance_evidence: Signed from 49ers as interior depth to supplement John Franklin-Myers and Jeffery Simmons after trading T'Vondre Sweat. Journeyman DT who has bounced from Browns to 49ers to Titans. Solid rotational piece but not a starter on most rosters.

trade_value.reasoning: Rotational DT on a 1-year deal. Minimal trade value — worth a conditional 7th at most. Depth piece, not an asset.

players.availability_reasoning: 1-year deal, depth signing. Would move him for any reasonable return.

players.name: Jeffery Simmons
players.performance_evidence: Dominant 2025: career-high 11 sacks (1st among IDL per PFF), 67 tackles, 17 TFL, 3 forced fumbles, 60 QB pressures. PFF 88.3 overall grade (3rd/134 DIs), 91.4 pass-rush grade (1st). Led all interior DL in sacks, pressure rate, and pass-rush win rate. Named PFF First-Team All-Pro, Pro Bowl selection (4th career). Best player on the team by a wide margin.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite interior defensive lineman coming off a career year — the best pass-rushing DT in football in 2025. Age 29 with 2 years left, but the remaining guarantees have vested away. Contract is heavy ($25.5M hit) but fair for All-Pro production. Worth a 1st-round pick to a contender. Spotrac projects a 3-year, $94M extension ($31.3M AAV).

players.availability_reasoning: GM Borgonzi rejected trade deadline interest and kept Simmons through the rebuild. Spotrac says the Titans need to 'fully compensate their best player' like Cleveland did with Garrett. Extension discussions likely. However, if the rebuild stalls and a contender offers multiple premium picks, the Titans would have to consider it. The vested guarantees expiring creates a theoretical trade window.
players.injury_notes: Played 15 of 17 games in 2025. Missed games in 2023 (knee). Generally durable.
players.trade_buzz: Other team executives estimated a 2nd or 3rd-round return at the 2025 draft deadline. Borgonzi did not entertain offers. Beat reporter Kuharsky reported teams 'haven't gotten the sense that Simmons is on the market.' Extension talks expected.

players.name: John Franklin-Myers
players.performance_evidence: Back-to-back productive seasons with Broncos. Career-high 7.5 sacks in 2025, though pressure rate dipped to 11% (below career average). Versatile — can rush from DE and interior. Reunites with Saleh from their Jets days. Expected to pair with Simmons on the DL.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid starter on a new 3-year deal. Reunites with Saleh in a scheme fit. $18M Year 1 cap hit is reasonable for his role. Worth a mid-to-late 2nd, but dead cap ($22M) makes a trade impossible in Year 1.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed to a 3-year, $63M deal specifically for Saleh's defense. $22M dead cap. Zero chance of a trade.

players.name: Cedric Gray
players.performance_evidence: Had a 'breakout season' per beat reporters in 2025. Earned a significant starting role in his 2nd year. Coaches spotlighted his play multiple times during the season. Part of the young defensive core being built around Simmons.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, ascending LB on a cheap 3rd-round rookie deal. 2 years of control remaining. Breakout 2025 puts him in the quality starter range. Worth a 3rd-4th round pick but the Titans need LB depth.

players.availability_reasoning: Core defensive building block on a cheap deal. Fits the rebuild timeline perfectly. Would need an overpay to move him.

players.name: James Williams Sr.
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who earned a significant role in 2025 alongside Cedric Gray. Started as a core special teamer and worked his way into the defensive rotation. Part of the young LB corps that beat reporters have praised for development.

trade_value.reasoning: UDFA on a minimum contract with 1 year left. Has shown enough to earn a role but limited proven production. Worth a conditional 7th at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth LB on a minimum deal. Would trade for any pick, but limited market demand.

players.name: Cor'Dale Flott
players.performance_evidence: Signed from Giants on a 3-year, $45M deal with $32M fully guaranteed. Started 14 games in 2025, had 1 INT and career-high 11 pass deflections. Long corner capable of playing outside and nickel. Ranked 42nd in yards per target allowed among qualified CBs — below average but not terrible. Part of Titans' massive CB investment.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old CB on a new 3-year deal. Decent starter but not a shutdown corner. The guaranteed money ($32M) locks the Titans in. Worth a mid-to-late 2nd based on age and contract, but dead cap ($24M) makes a trade impossible.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed to a 3-year, $45M deal. $24M dead cap. Not going anywhere.

players.name: Alontae Taylor
players.performance_evidence: Signed from Saints on a 3-year, $60M deal with $42M guaranteed. Physical corner who can blitz (4 sacks in 2024, 2 in 2025). Fast, in-your-face style fits Saleh's aggressive defensive mindset. Ranked 41st in yards per target allowed — below average but physical. Returns to home state of Tennessee.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old CB on a big new deal. Physically gifted with blitzing upside. $42M guaranteed makes this a firm commitment. Worth a 2nd-round pick on talent but $32M dead cap makes a trade impossible.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed to a 3-year, $60M deal. $32M dead cap. Cornerstone of the rebuilt secondary.

players.name: Kevin Winston Jr.
players.performance_evidence: 3rd-round pick in 2025. Season cut short by injury — placed on IR alongside Xavier Woods and Mike Brown. Limited sample to evaluate. Listed as nickel/safety hybrid. The Titans signed him to his rookie deal alongside the other 2025 picks. Expected to compete for a larger role in 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Young 3rd-round pick on a cheap deal with 3 years of control. Injury limited his rookie year, so value is based on draft pedigree and projection. Worth a 5th-round pick as a developmental piece.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap, part of the rebuild. Need to see what he can do healthy before making him available. Premium required.
players.injury_notes: Placed on season-ending IR in 2025 (knee). Recovery timeline unclear.

players.name: Amani Hooker
players.performance_evidence: Signed a 3-year, $40.5M extension just before the 2025 season. 80 career games, 315 tackles, 12 INTs, 31 PDs. Team captain and one of the most consistent players on the roster. 12 INTs rank 9th in Titans era among all players, 4th among safeties. Missed some time in 2025 (ruled out vs multiple opponents) but remains a leader.

trade_value.reasoning: Reliable starting safety on a freshly signed 3-year extension. Age 27, in his prime window. Good but not elite — more of a quality starter than Pro Bowl player. Worth a 2nd-round pick but high dead cap ($15M) makes a trade very unlikely.

players.availability_reasoning: Just extended. Team captain. But the Titans are rebuilding and Hooker is one of the older core pieces. If a contender offered a 2nd-round pick, the Titans might listen, especially if Winston Jr. develops. Not being shopped but has a price.
players.injury_notes: Missed games in 2025. Spotrac notes he's 'fully guaranteed through 2026' — his salary was locked in.

players.name: Kendell Brooks
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who worked his way into a starting FS role after injuries decimated the safety room in 2025. Limited track record as a full-time starter. The Titans' depth at safety was so thin they had to rely on young, unproven players after Hooker, Woods, Winston, and Brown all missed significant time.

trade_value.reasoning: UDFA on a minimum deal who started by default due to injuries. No demonstrated high-end ability. Worth a conditional late-round pick at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Placeholder starter. If the Titans add a FS in the draft or FA, Brooks returns to a depth role. Would move for any pick.

players.name: Cordell Volson
players.performance_evidence: Started at guard for the Bengals across the 2022-2024 seasons, grading as a below-average to average pass protector per PFF. Allowed a higher-than-ideal pressure rate but was durable and available. Moved to Tennessee as a depth/competition piece; projects as a low-end starter or swing IOL option. Not a player who elevates the position but holds down a spot.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average starting guard on a cheap expiring deal. Minimal trade value — a 7th-round pick swap at best. Teams can find equivalent IOL depth easily.

players.availability_reasoning: Journeyman guard on a final-year minimum-level deal. Tennessee has no reason to hold him if a team offers any draft compensation. Not a core piece.

players.name: Jermaine Johnson
players.performance_evidence: Showed flashes with the Jets — 7.5 sacks in 2023 before tearing his Achilles in the early portion of the 2024 season. When healthy, he displayed good bend and run defense as a 4-3 DE. The Achilles tear is a significant red flag; edge rushers coming off Achilles injuries have a mixed track record. Prior to the injury, he was trending toward quality starter/fringe Pro Bowl territory but hadn't yet produced a dominant season. Acquired by Tennessee in the reshuffling after the Jets' roster dissolution.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 1st-round pick still on a cheap rookie-scale deal with 2 years of control. The Achilles injury significantly depresses value, but if he returns to 2023 form, this is a steal. Worth a mid-3rd-round pick equivalent — teams are buying upside and contract value against injury risk.

players.availability_reasoning: Tennessee likely views Johnson as a high-upside, low-cost edge rusher on a team-friendly deal. They'd listen to offers but would want a 2nd-round pick given the contract value. If he looks healthy in camp, price goes up. If he struggles in recovery, could become more available.
players.injury_notes: Torn Achilles suffered early in the 2024 season. Recovery timeline should have him available for 2026 but he'll be less than 2 full years removed from the injury. Edge rushers historically have mixed results post-Achilles.
players.trade_buzz: No significant buzz yet. Teams will likely inquire during training camp once his health can be evaluated in person.

players.name: Jacob Martin
players.performance_evidence: Career journeyman pass rusher/linebacker who has bounced between multiple teams (Texans, Jets, Panthers, Titans). Career sack totals are modest — never topped 5.5 sacks in a season. Has primarily been a rotational edge/off-ball hybrid who contributes on special teams. Moving into LB2 likely reflects Tennessee's thin linebacker corps rather than Martin's standout play. Below-average against the run, provides occasional pass-rush value.

trade_value.reasoning: Aging rotational player on an expiring deal with no guaranteed money. Essentially no trade value — a conditional 7th at most. Teams can find equivalent production in free agency or off waivers.

players.availability_reasoning: Veteran depth piece on a one-year deal with no dead cap. If Tennessee drafts or signs a linebacker, Martin becomes expendable immediately. Would be released before traded in most scenarios.

players.name: Micah Robinson
players.performance_evidence: Young, unproven corner who earned a starting role through strong camp and preseason performances. Limited sample size as a starter — may have been thrust into CB2 due to roster turnover and lack of depth rather than dominant play. Physical profile is intriguing (good size and length for an outside corner), but he hasn't faced a full season of NFL-caliber targets. Expect growing pains but developmental upside as a UDFA who worked his way up.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cheap, and under team control, which helps, but unproven UDFA corners starting out of necessity have minimal trade value. Worth a 5th-round pick equivalent — teams would want to see more sustained production before paying a significant price.

players.availability_reasoning: Tennessee likely values Robinson's cheap deal and youth. They won't give him away, but if a team offered a 4th-round pick, it would be hard to turn down for an unproven UDFA. Team would prefer to develop him unless they get a pick they consider above his projected ceiling.

players.name: Tony Adams
players.performance_evidence: Former UDFA who developed with the Jets and earned meaningful playing time as a box safety/strong safety. Good run support and physicality near the line of scrimmage. Limited range in deep coverage, which caps his ceiling. Adequate starter in a defense that doesn't ask the SS to cover a lot of ground. Around 70-80 tackles per season when starting, with occasional splash plays. PFF grades have been in the 55-62 range — functional but unspectacular.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average starting safety on a cheap expiring deal. Safety is a low-value position in trade markets, and Adams doesn't have the coverage ability to command a premium. Worth a late 6th-round pick equivalent at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Expiring deal, no dead cap, and safety is a replaceable position. Tennessee would move Adams for any reasonable pick. If they draft or sign a safety, he becomes immediately expendable.
